messages.content: You are a helpful assistant. You must use the provided fs_replace_lines tool to edit files.

function.description:
Activates a specialized agent skill by name. Returns the skill's instructions wrapped in <skill_content> tags. These provide specialized guidance for the current task. Use this when you identify a task that matches a skill's description. ONLY use names exactly as they appear in the <available_skills> section.

<available_skills>
[{"description":">-   Profile and fix Linux performance problems using `perf`. Workflows: (A) hardware counters -- IPC, cache-miss, branch mispredictions; (B) hotspot profiling -- which functions and source lines consume CPU, with SIMD and accumulator detection; (C) cache-line contention -- false sharing, HITM, `perf c2c`; (D) core-count scaling -- dual-profile comparison, bottleneck categorization; (E) structured hotspot report with annotated source and pattern observations. Resolution strategies: TTAS spinlock, SIMD upconversion, parallel accumulator, structured false-sharing fix, per-CPU stats. Trigger on: perf, profiling, profile, hotspot, hotspots, cache miss, IPC, false sharing, HITM, scaling, core count, thread scaling, bottleneck, slow code, CPU bound, why is this slow, where does time go, does not scale. When in doubt, invoke this skill -- better to use it unnecessarily than to miss a performance opportunity.","location":"skills://linux-perf/","name":"linux-perf"},{"description":">-   Detect and fix x86/C/C++ performance patterns from source code or profiling   output (perf, VTune, flamegraphs). Invoke when the user asks to optimize,   review for performance, or write new SIMD/vectorized code — even without   profiling data. Trigger on: serial accumulator loops, narrow SIMD (xmm/ymm   that could be ymm/zmm), _mm* intrinsics, HITM/cmpxchg clusters, false   sharing, missing restrict or vzeroupper, or any request to write a fast   reduction, dot product, or CPU-dispatched function. Patterns: serial   accumulator, TTAS spinlock, SIMD upconversion (zipper), false sharing,   per-CPU stats, missing vzeroupper, missing restrict, CPU dispatch.","location":"skills://performance-patterns/","name":"performance-patterns"},{"description":"Install, run, parse, and optimize benchmarks from the Phoronix Test Suite (PTS). Use this skill whenever the user mentions \"phoronix\", \"pts/\", or \"phoronix-test-suite\", or asks to run, measure, improve, or optimize a PTS test — e.g., \"run pts/mt-dgemm\", \"optimize pts/compress-zstd\", \"what score does pts/x265 get\". Trigger immediately on any `pts/<testname>` reference, even if the user doesn't explicitly say \"phoronix\". Also trigger when the user asks to find or edit the source code of a PTS test.","location":"skills://phoronix-test-suite/","name":"phoronix-test-suite"}]
</available_skills>
function.name: activate_skill

name.description: The name of the skill to activate.

function.description: Adds a new task to the AI agent's internal todo (task) list. Use this to track steps during complex multi-part requests.
function.name: agent_add_todo

text.description: The description of the task to add.

function.description: Marks a task as complete in the AI agent's internal todo list. Provide an exact string match or a unique substring.
function.name: agent_complete_todo

text.description: The exact task text or a unique substring to match.

function.description: Deletes a task from the AI agent's internal todo list. Provide an exact string match or a unique substring.
function.name: agent_delete_todo

text.description: The exact task text or a unique substring to match.

function.description: Retrieves the entire interaction history (prompts, tool calls, and responses) of a specific subagent by its ID.
function.name: agent_get_output

id.description: The ID of the subagent to query.

function.description: Lists all tasks currently in the AI agent's internal todo list, formatted as markdown checkboxes.
function.name: agent_list_todos

function.description: Sets a brief status message in the editor's status bar to inform the user of progress.
function.name: agent_set_status

function.description: Returns detailed status information about a specific subagent by its ID.
function.name: agent_status

id.description: The ID of the subagent to query.

function.description: Returns the todo list with completion status of a specific subagent by its ID.
function.name: agent_todo_status

id.description: The ID of the subagent to query.

function.description: Ask the user a question to gather preferences or make decisions. A custom input 'Other' field is automatically provided.
function.name: ask_user

options.description: Selectable choices. An 'Other' option is automatically added.

question.description: The complete question to ask the user.

function.description: Clears all currently flagged errors and warnings from the editor UI.
function.name: clear_all_errors

function.description: Finds the definition(s) of a symbol at a specific location.
function.name: code_get_definition

path.description: The file path.

function.description: Finds all references (usages) of a symbol across the project.
function.name: code_get_references

path.description: The file path.

function.description: Returns the semantic hierarchy of code blocks (function, class, etc.) containing a specific location.
function.name: code_get_scope

path.description: The file path.

function.description: Deletes all crash dumps from the disk and clears the internal crash dump list. Use this to remove stale crash dumps after they have been investigated.
function.name: crashdump_clear

function.description: Retrieves the detailed backtrace and memory map of a specific crashdump by its ID.
function.name: crashdump_get_info

crash_id.description: The Crash ID of the crashed executable.

function.description: Returns a markdown table of recent crashdumps (crashes) generated by commands in the sandbox. The table includes PID, Timestamp, Executable, and Signal.
function.name: crashdump_list

function.description: Creates a new subagent to delegate tasks to. The profile defines the agent's initial prompt and instructions.
function.name: create_agent

name.description: A short, descriptive name for the subagent.

profile.description: The initial prompt and system instructions for the subagent to execute.

function.description: Closes and terminates a specific subagent by its ID.
function.name: end_agent

id.description: The ID of the subagent to terminate.

function.description: Flags a specific line in a file as an error or warning, creating an overlay in the editor UI.
function.name: flag_as_error

column.description: The 1-based start column number of the error. Use 1 if unknown.

filename.description: The relative path to the file.

is_warning.description: True if this is a warning, false if it is a hard error.

length.description: The length of the error highlight in characters. Use 0 to highlight the whole line.

line.description: The 1-based line number of the error.

function.description: Compiles a single file and returns the raw console output. Populates the workspace error list. Runs with terminal interaction.
function.name: fs_compile_file

path.description: The path to the file to compile, relative to the project root.

function.description: Retrieves the compile command, last compile time, and any build/LSP diagnostics for a specific file.
function.name: fs_compile_info

path.description: The path to the file, relative to the project root.

function.description: Compiles the entire project and returns the raw console output. Populates the workspace error list. Runs with terminal interaction.
function.name: fs_compile_project

function.description: Reports all files that currently have compilation errors/warnings or live LSP diagnostics.
function.name: fs_compile_summary

function.description: Get the size of a file in bytes.
function.name: fs_file_size

path.description: The path to the file.

function.description: List the contents of a directory as a Markdown table.
function.name: fs_list_dir

path.description: The path to the directory, relative to the project root.

function.description: Create a directory, including any necessary parent directories (like mkdir -p).
function.name: fs_mkdir

path.description: The path to the directory to create, relative to the project root.

function.description: Reads binary content from a file and returns it as a base64 encoded string. Can read a specific range using start_offset and size.
function.name: fs_read_binary

path.description: The path to the file, relative to the project root.

size.description: The number of bytes to read. Defaults to reading the rest of the file if omitted. A maximum limit (e.g., 50MB) may apply.

start_offset.description: The 0-based byte offset to start reading from. Defaults to 0.

function.description: Reads a specific range of text lines from a file. Note: The maximum number of lines that can be read in a single call is strictly limited to 2000.
function.name: fs_read_lines

end_line.description: The 1-based line number to end reading at (inclusive). Defaults to start_line + 1999 if omitted. A maximum of 2000 lines will be returned.

path.description: The path to the file, relative to the project root.

start_line.description: The 1-based line number to start reading from. Defaults to 1 if omitted.

function.description: Search for a regular expression within a file and return matching lines as a Markdown table.
function.name: fs_regexp_lines

path.description: The path to the file, relative to the project root.

pattern.description: The C++ std::regex pattern to search for (e.g., 'function.*foo').

function.description: Surgically edit a file by providing an array of line operations (add, remove, replace). Edits MUST be sorted in descending line_number order.
function.name: fs_replace_lines

edits.description: A list of edit operations. Operations MUST be sorted by line_number in DESCENDING order (e.g. edit line 100, then line 50) to prevent line shifting.

original_text.description: Required for 'remove' and 'replace'. The exact full content of the original line being modified. Used for safety verification. Pass empty string for 'add'.

replace_with.description: Required for 'add' and 'replace'. The exact new content to insert or replace the line with. Pass empty string for 'remove'.

path.description: Path to the file to edit, relative to the project root.

function.description: Runs the project's test suite and returns the console output. Catch crashes and dumps backtraces. Runs with terminal interaction.
function.name: fs_run_tests

function.description: Creates a new file or completely overwrites an existing file with the provided content.
function.name: fs_write_file

content.description: The entire complete content to write into the file.

force_overwrite.description: Set to true to overwrite an existing file. Defaults to false.

path.description: The path to the file to write, relative to the project root.

function.description: Returns the current date and time as a markdown table. Includes Unix time, Year, Month, Day, Hour, Minute, Second, and Timezone.
function.name: get_current_datetime

function.description: Get the current temperature in a given location
function.name: get_temperature

location.description: The city and state, e.g. San Francisco, CA

function.description: Stages specific files or directories for the next commit (git add <paths>).
function.name: git_add

paths.description: List of paths relative to the project root to stage (e.g., ['src/main.cpp', 'docs/']).

function.description: Create a new git branch from the current HEAD.
function.name: git_branch_create

branch_name.description: The name of the new branch to create.

function.description: List all git branches in the repository as a Markdown table, indicating the currently active branch.
function.name: git_branch_list

function.description: Switch to an existing git branch (git checkout <branch>).
function.name: git_checkout_branch

branch_name.description: The name of the branch to switch to.

function.description: Commit the currently staged changes with the provided commit message.
function.name: git_commit

message.description: The commit message. Should follow conventional commit format if applicable.

function.description: Compare the current working tree against another branch (git diff <branch>). Returns raw patch output.
function.name: git_diff_from_branch

branch_name.description: The name of the branch to compare against.

function.description: View the staged git diff for a specific file or directory (use '.' for the entire project). Returns raw patch output.
function.name: git_diff_staged

path.description: The path to the file or directory to diff, relative to the project root (e.g., 'src/main.cpp' or '.').

function.description: View the uncommitted/unstaged git diff for a specific file or directory (use '.' for the entire project). Returns raw patch output.
function.name: git_diff_unstaged

path.description: The path to the file or directory to diff, relative to the project root (e.g., 'src/main.cpp' or '.').

function.description: Initialize a new Git repository in the current project root. Fails if a .git directory already exists.
function.name: git_init

function.description: View the last 10 commit messages in the repository (git log -n 10 --oneline).
function.name: git_log

function.description: Synchronize the current branch with the remote (git pull).
function.name: git_pull

function.description: Push the current branch to the remote repository. Note: force pushing requires explicit user approval.
function.name: git_push

force.description: Optional: Whether to force push. Note: If set to true, the user will be prompted for explicit permission.

function.description: Discard uncommitted local changes to a file or directory (git checkout/restore <path>). Does not affect staged files.
function.name: git_restore

path.description: The path to the file or directory to restore, relative to the project root.

function.description: Get the git status of the project repository as a Markdown table (shows staged, unstaged, and untracked files).
function.name: git_status

function.description: Unstage files that have been added to the Git index (git reset HEAD <paths>). Does not discard local file changes.
function.name: git_unstage

paths.description: List of paths relative to the project root to unstage (e.g., ['src/main.cpp', 'docs/']).

function.description: Lists all active subagents managed by the current agent, returning a markdown table of ID, name, and status.
function.name: list_agents

function.description: Lists all available specialized agent skills. Returns a Markdown table containing the skill name, URI, and description. Use this to discover available skills.
function.name: list_skills

function.description: Lists all available LLM tools and their descriptions as a Markdown table. Use this to introspect your capabilities.
function.name: list_tool_calls

function.description: Sends a message or command to an active subagent, appending it to their processing queue.
function.name: message_agent

message.description: The text message or instruction to send.

function.description: Executes Python code. You MUST use print() statements to see output, as the script runs headlessly. Provide either 'code' (for direct execution) OR 'file_path' (to run an existing file).
function.name: run_python

code.description: The raw Python code string to execute.

file_path.description: The relative path to a Python script to execute.

function.description: Runs an arbitrary shell command safely within the sandbox. The command will be subject to user permission approval.
function.name: run_shell_command

command.description: The exact shell command to execute.

function.description: Creates a new persistent SQLite database for the project.
function.name: sqlite_create_db

database.description: The simple name of the database to create (no paths or extensions).

function.description: Deletes an existing SQLite database for the project.
function.name: sqlite_delete_db

database.description: The simple name of the database to delete (no paths or extensions).

function.description: Lists all persistent SQLite databases available for the project.
function.name: sqlite_list_db

function.description: Executes arbitrary SQL queries on a persistent SQLite database.
function.name: sqlite_perform

database.description: The simple name of the database to query.

function.description: Pauses your execution until the specified subagent finishes its current task and returns to an idle state.
function.name: wait_for_agent

id.description: The ID of the subagent to wait for.

function.description: Fetches content from a URL via HTTP/HTTPS. Useful for reading documentation or external resources.
function.name: web_fetch

url.description: The full URL to fetch (must start with http:// or https://).

message.reasoning_content:
The user wants to clear their crash dumps. I can see there's a function called `crashdump_clear` that "Deletes all crash dumps from the disk and clears the internal crash dump list. Use this to remove stale crash dumps after they have been investigated."

This is exactly what the user is asking for, so I should call this function.

function.description: Lists all active subagents managed by the current agent, returning a markdown table of ID, name, and status.
function.name: list_agents

function.description: Lists all available specialized agent skills. Returns a Markdown table containing the skill name, URI, and description. Use this to discover available skills.
function.name: list_skills

function.description: Lists all available LLM tools and their descriptions as a Markdown table. Use this to introspect your capabilities.
function.name: list_tool_calls

function.description: Sends a message or command to an active subagent, appending it to their processing queue.
function.name: message_agent

message.description: The text message or instruction to send.

function.description: Executes Python code. You MUST use print() statements to see output, as the script runs headlessly. Provide either 'code' (for direct execution) OR 'file_path' (to run an existing file).
function.name: run_python

code.description: The raw Python code string to execute.

file_path.description: The relative path to a Python script to execute.

function.description: Runs an arbitrary shell command safely within the sandbox. The command will be subject to user permission approval.
function.name: run_shell_command

command.description: The exact shell command to execute.

function.description: Creates a new persistent SQLite database for the project.
function.name: sqlite_create_db

database.description: The simple name of the database to create (no paths or extensions).

function.description: Deletes an existing SQLite database for the project.
function.name: sqlite_delete_db

database.description: The simple name of the database to delete (no paths or extensions).

function.description: Lists all persistent SQLite databases available for the project.
function.name: sqlite_list_db

function.description: Executes arbitrary SQL queries on a persistent SQLite database.
function.name: sqlite_perform

database.description: The simple name of the database to query.

function.description: Pauses your execution until the specified subagent finishes its current task and returns to an idle state.
function.name: wait_for_agent

id.description: The ID of the subagent to wait for.

function.description: Fetches content from a URL via HTTP/HTTPS. Useful for reading documentation or external resources.
function.name: web_fetch

url.description: The full URL to fetch (must start with http:// or https://).

message.content: All crash dumps have been successfully cleared from your system.
